creator: gnb@itga.com.au
text:
The conserver program has a somewhat fractured history.

There is a port for conserver-8.5 in comms/conserver.  This is an
older and somewhat idiosyncratic version maintained by Kevin S
Braunsdorf <ksb+conserver@sa.fedex.com>.  It requires a couple of
large packages to compile.  It fails to authenticate against MD5
passwords (see PR ports/28432
http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=28432) and doesn't
interoperate with Solaris very easily.  But it does support remote power
controllers.

7.0.2 is a more recent and cleaner version supported by Bryan Stansell 
<bryan@conserver.com>, and is based on earlier versions of Kevin's code.
This doesn't need any extra build packages, and has a couple of extra features
(consoles on terminal server ports, local password file), and compiles
and runs cleanly on recent FreeBSD and Solaris.

For more detailed comparison, see
	http://www.itga.com.au/~gnb/console/index.html#conserver-versions

Fix: The following ports framework can be used either to replace the
existing conserger port or to add a second alternative version.
